items.title: Murder charge for Singaporean in JB court over death of fellow citizen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 25-year-old Singaporean man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today for the murder of a fellow countryman in Southkey here last month. The accused, Muhammad Arif Saiyadi, understood the charge read out by the court interpreter, however no plea was recorded before Magistrate Nabilah Nizam. According to the charge sheet, the man was charged with murder by causing the death of Chia Hon Chung, 32, at Persiaran Southkey 1, Kota Southkey here at 5.30am on March 14.

items.title: Broad-based SME stimulus not needed for now, says Bank Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has indicated that broad-based stimulus measures are not immediately necessary to support small and medium enterprises (SMEs), favouring targeted assistance instead. Responding to calls from industry groups for government intervention amid geopolitical uncertainties, the central bank said Malaysia is entering the current environment from a "position of strength". Speaking at Bank Negara's Annual Report 2025 press conference, governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said businesses have already undergone significant support measures following the pandemic.

items.title: DoE raids illegal plastic recycling premises in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: The Kedah Department of Environment (DoE) raided a premises suspected of carrying out illegal plastic waste processing activities in the Taman Ria Jaya Industrial Area here yesterday. She said inspections found the premises engaged in cleaning and extrusion of plastic waste to produce plastic pellets, and operating without the required Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) approval under the Environmental Quality Act 1974. "DoE also collected samples of plastic waste and pellets from the premises for laboratory analysis by the Chemistry department," she said.

items.title: Malaysia seeks deeper Japan ties, boosts semiconductor push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is seeking deeper industrial cooperation with Japan, particularly in the semiconductor sector, Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Sim Tze Tzin said. "Malaysia has signed 17 free trade agreements, making Malaysia a very neutral ground to access a very wide market in the world for Japanese companies," he said at the New Japan-Malaysia Industrial Cooperation Seminar today. Sim added that Malaysia's industrial policy demonstrates strong alignment with Japan's strategic priorities, particularly across 17 strategic fields, including semiconductors, green transformation and economic security.

items.title: Miti clarifies BYD license terms, says rules apply to all investors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) has clarified that conditions imposed on Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker BYD's proposed assembly operations in Malaysia are part of a broader, non-discriminatory policy framework. The company's local unit, BYD Automotive Malaysia Sdn Bhd, was granted an interim manufacturing licence on Sept 29, 2025 to assemble energy efficient vehicles, including EVs and plug-in hybrid models, in Tanjung Malim, Perak. "These conditions are not unique to BYD but reflect a consistent approach applied to all new automotive investments in Malaysia," it said.

items.title: Malaysia condemns attack on Unifil, extends condolences to Indonesia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has conveyed its condolences to the family and the Government of Indonesia following an incident that killed one peacekeeper and injured three others serving under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil). In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said any attack on a Unifil peacekeeping mission constituted a serious violation of international humanitarian law and United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701 (2006). In a related development, the Foreign Ministry said personnel from the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) currently serving under the Unifil mission were safe.

items.title: Yeo's cuts Singapore jobs as production shifts to Malaysia on rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: HOME-grown beverage giant Yeo Hiap Seng is laying off 25 employees at its Senoko facility - about nine per cent of its Singapore workforce - as it consolidates its canned food manufacturing operations in Malaysia. "This consolidation enables the group's Johor and Selangor facilities to optimise capacity utilisation and strengthen overall manufacturing efficiency across its network," said the mainboard-listed company in a Singapore stock exchange filing on Tuesday, The move comes as Singapore's food and beverage sector grapples with rising input costs, driven by higher energy, logistics and raw material prices, alongside geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran conflict. Yeo's said the Senoko layoffs were communicated during a closed-door briefing at its 3 Senoko Way facility.

items.title: Kota Setar, Pendang schools to resume face-to-face classes tomorrow
items.summary: ALOR STAR: School sessions in Kota Setar and Pendang will resume face-to-face tomorrow after both districts recorded Level 1 (Alert) temperature readings of between 35 degrees Celsius and 37 degrees Celsius since yesterday. Kedah Education Department (JPN) director Abdul Rahim Mat said the department continuously monitored temperature readings issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) before deciding on the implementation of home-based teaching and learning (PdPR) in the state. He added that with temperatures now easing, schools would resume normal operations, although all activities would be conducted in line with guidelines issued to schools, parents and students.

items.title: Villagers build sandbags as Golok River dries up in drought
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Residents along the Malaysia–Thailand border are building sandbag barriers on the Golok River to retain water as drought leaves levels dangerously low. With the river dropping to knee height, villagers must rely on it for bathing, washing, and collecting water for daily needs. Life revolves around the river after his home has been without a water supply for three months.

items.title: SPM 2025 leavers urged to join PISMP to become teachers by 2030
items.summary: KAJANG: Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 leavers who meet the entry requirements are encouraged to continue their studies through the Bachelor of Education (PISMP) programme at Teacher Education Institutes (IPG) nationwide. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said applications for the 2026 intake were still open and will close this Thursday. For Sekolah Menengah Jenis Kebangsaan (SMJK), we, together with Dong Zong (United Chinese School Committees' Association) and Jiao Zong (United Chinese School Teachers' Association), are actively promoting this opportunity.

items.title: Korean Air to shift to emergency mode in April amid rising oil prices from Iran war
items.summary: SEOUL: Korean Air will enter emergency management mode from April as rising oil prices driven by the war in the Middle East weigh on costs, a source with knowledge of the matter told Reuters on Tuesday. T'way Air and Asiana Airlines have also entered emergency management mode this month amid rising fuel costs, according to local media reports, underscoring the broader strain on South Korea's aviation industry. Korean Air said it expects fuel costs for April to rise to around 450 US cents per gallon, significantly higher than the 220 US cents per gallon cited in its business plan, a sharp increase in its monthly cost burden.

items.title: Bank Negara: Malaysian banks well-capitalised, can withstand crisis-level shocks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's financial system remains robust and well-positioned to support the economy despite global uncertainties, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said. He said banks and insurers in Malaysia continue to operate with strong capital positions and ample liquidity. He said credit flows remain intact, supporting working capital and investment needs, reflecting the financial system's role in facilitating growth rather than amplifying economic stress.

items.title: Hot weather: Mobile pumps deployed to Perak padi fields
items.summary: IPOH: Mobile water pumps have been deployed as a temporary measure to ensure an adequate water supply in several critical padi cultivation areas in Perak amid the ongoing hot weather. "Mobile water pumps are currently maintaining water supply in the affected areas, but their long-term effectiveness will depend on existing water sources, particularly rivers," he said. He also said that the Seberang Perak IADA had submitted requests to borrow additional mobile water pumps from relevant agencies to ensure all affected areas receive sufficient water supply.

items.title: Pontian municipal officer pleads not guilty to cheating charges involving RM62,760 over building application
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 38 year old municipal administrator claimed trial in the Sessions Court here today on a charge of deceiving a man into handing over a cheque and cash totalling RM62,760 to speed up the application for the construction of a two storey building in Rimba Terjun, Pontian, over the past five years. The accused, Muhammad Nazri Abu Bakar, who is attached to the Pontian Municipal Council (MPPn), pleaded not guilty to five charges after they were read out by an interpreter before Judge Datuk Ahmad Kamal Arifin Ismail. Based on the first to fifth charges, the accused is charged with deceiving a 72-year-old man into handing over three cheques and cash totalling RM62,760.

items.title: Thai woman caught smuggling 51kg of subsidised cooking oil in car spare tyre
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A Thai woman's attempt to smuggle 51kg of Malaysian subsidised cooking oil, concealed in a vehicle's spare tyre compartment, was foiled when she was arrested yesterday. Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry deputy director Aswadi Jaafar said the woman was stopped at 2.15pm during Ops Tiris. He said authorities would also determine the source of the subsidised cooking oil obtained by the suspect.

items.title: Matrade, Responsible Business Alliance partner to advance ESG-driven trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: THE Malaysia External Trade Development Corp (Matrade) has partnered with the Responsible Business Alliance (RBA) to advance responsible and sustainable trade practices. By leveraging their respective networks and expertise, Matrade and RBA will work together to enhance awareness, facilitate timely information sharing and strengthen the understanding of evolving global sustainability requirements among stakeholders. The collaboration will also support broader outreach efforts to industry players, encouraging greater adoption of responsible business practices across global supply chains.

items.title: Five Orang Asli students achieve straight A's in SPM 2025
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results saw five Orang Asli candidates achieve outstanding results, earning straight A+, A, and A- grades in all subjects. Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said a total of 133 Orang Asli candidates obtained at least a credit, with grades ranging from B+ to C. "In addition, one candidate from Sekolah Integriti and Henry Gurney School achieved straight A grades, while 24 candidates earned at least a credit and 63 passed with minimum grades. Azam added that 1,768 Orang Asli candidates qualified for the SPM 2025 certificate, compared with 1,527 last year.

items.title: South Korea proposes US$17.3bil extra budget to mitigate Middle East shock
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea proposed on Tuesday a supplementary government budget of US$17.3 billion to support consumers and companies hit by an escalating Middle East war. A surge in oil prices sparked by the US-Israeli war on Iran has raised growth and inflation risks for Asia's fourth-largest economy, which is also the world's fourth-largest importer of oil, with 70 per cent of its purchases coming from the Middle East. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Cargill boosts Port Klang plant with new line amid rising Asia-Pacific demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cargill has announced a multi-million-dollar expansion of its edible oil plant in Port Klang, Malaysia, adding a new speciality fats production line. The move aims to strengthen the company's portfolio and offer customers a broader range of ingredients for chocolate confectionery, bakery, and dairy products tailored to diverse market and consumer needs. Cargill's Food Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand vice president and managing director Kashan Rashid said the new production line at the Port Klang facility supports customers with reliable access to high-quality, versatile speciality fats.

items.title: Man arrested for knife attack and motorcycle arson
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Police have arrested a man suspected of injuring another person with a knife before setting the victim's motorcycle ablaze at Jalan Singkir Badong, near Yan, yesterday. Yan police chief Superintendent Mohamad Hamizi Abdullah said the incident occurred at 7pm and involved a 34-year-old motorcyclist and a 41-year-old suspect. The suspect then removed the petrol pipe and set the motorcycle on fire," he said in a statement today.

items.title: SPM 2025: Record 366,435 candidates qualify for certificate
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 366,435 candidates, or 94.29 per cent, qualified for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 certificate, up from 355,933 candidates, or 94.01 per cent, in 2024. "For candidates from Education Ministry (MoE) schools, 329,272 candidates, or 94.51 per cent, qualified in 2025, compared with 319,016 candidates, or 94.20 per cent, in 2024," he said. Azam said 1,878 candidates with special educational needs sat for the 2025 examination, with 1,516, or 80.72 per cent, qualifying, compared with 1,377, or 78.73 per cent, in 2024, marking an increase of 139 candidates, or 1.99 per cent.

items.title: SPM 2025: BM dips while Islamic Education soars
items.summary: Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said 63 subjects showed improved performance, while three remained unchanged compared with SPM 2024. Among the seven core subjects, three recorded gains, while four saw a drop in performance. "Among 12 STEM elective subjects in Applied Science and Technology, eight showed improved performance.

items.title: Kelantan schools suspend outdoor activities amid dry spell
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Education Department has instructed all 595 primary and secondary schools in the state to temporarily suspend outdoor activities amid the ongoing dry spell. Norina said all schools had also been advised to closely monitor daily temperatures and follow guidelines issued by the Meteorological Department to prevent heat-related illnesses, including heatstroke. Norina said that temperatures of 39 to 40 degrees Celsius were classified as extreme.

items.title: Public urged to assist probe into voyeurism at Subang Jaya shopping mall
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Police have urged members of the public with information to come forward as they intensify efforts to track down a man suspected of recording videos under a woman's skirt at a shopping mall here. Subang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Wan Azlan Wan Mamat said a report was lodged at 1.16pm on March 29 after a video of the incident began circulating on social media. Wan Azlan said police are working to identify both the victim and the suspect.

items.title: Rafizi urged to quit PKR if he continues public attacks
items.summary: KUCHING: The statements and actions of Rafizi Ramli can no longer be taken lightly, says Sarawak Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) secretary Mahmud Epah. "They clearly show a pattern that is tarnishing PKR's image and undermining the noble aims of Reformasi. Mahmud added that Rafizi, the former PKR deputy president, should have used the party's democratic channels to communicate his views.

items.title: Penang records drop in SPM 2025 absenteeism as attendance improves
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang recorded a notable improvement in attendance for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 examination, with absenteeism falling compared with the previous year. Penang Education Department director Mohammad Dziauddin Mat Saad said a total of 20,057 students registered for SPM 2025, an increase of 804 candidates, or about four per cent, from 2024. Of these, 18,132 candidates attended the examination, reflecting strong participation.

items.title: Motorcyclist hurt in Yan knife attack, suspect torches bike in rage before Kedah police arrest him
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have arrested a man suspected of injuring another individual with a knife before setting the victim’s motorcycle ablaze at Jalan Singkir Badong, near Yan, yesterday. Yan Police chief Supt Mohamad Hamizi Abdullah said the incident occurred at about 7pm and involved a 34-year-old motorcyclist and a 41-year-old suspect. The suspect then removed the petrol pipe and set the motorcycle on fire,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Livestock farmers warn chicken prices could rise amid soaring diesel costs
items.summary: MELAKA: The Federation of Livestock Farmers' Associations of Malaysia (FLFAM) says that chicken prices may rise due to higher diesel costs, though the timing of any increase remains uncertain. According to Kosmo!, FLFAM advisor Datuk Jeffrey Ng said chicken prices in Melaka remained stable for now, as the country was still relying on existing supplies. "Currently, chicken prices are stable and affordable, but an increase is inevitable due to rising costs, particularly the significant rise in diesel prices.

items.title: Financial system remains resilient on strong regulatory framework – Bank Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's financial system remained resilient in 2025, supported by a prudent and robust regulatory environment, said Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara). To help financial institutions stay strong and well-prepared for shocks, Bank Negara has continued its efforts to align its regulatory framework with the Basel III standards. "This in turn protects depositors and supports confidence in the financial system," Bank Negara said in its Annual Report 2025.

items.title: Bank Negara processes 1.7bil banknotes in 2025, 86pct handled via automated cash centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) has processed 1.7 billion banknotes in 2025, compared with 2.5 billion in 2024, with 86 per cent handled through the automated cash centre (ACC) compared with 69 per cent in the previous year. "They complement Bank Negara's centralised operations and ensure timely distribution of banknotes to meet public demand," Bank Negara said in its Annual Report 2025 released here today. "Recirculation is a key component of a sustainable cash ecosystem," Bank Negara said.

items.title: Bank Negara focuses on value-based intermediation to advance Islamic financial system this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) is focusing on strengthening value-based intermediation (VBI) capabilities including in sustainability and climate agenda in Islamic financial system this year. "Guided by strategic stewardship and collaboration, Bank Negara remains committed to advancing Malaysia's Islamic financial ecosystem, to ensure its progressive evolution delivers lasting benefits for all," it said in its Annual Report 2025 released today. The central bank said its work centred on three key strategic thrusts in 2025, which are advancing value-based innovation, strengthening the Islamic finance ecosystem, and retaining Malaysia's position as a global leader in the field.

items.title: Knife attack at Kapar eatery leaves man wounded; police arrest suspect with prior offences
items.summary: North Klang District Police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said in the incident at about 6.09am, the complainant and the victim, his 69-year-old local friend, were approached by a group of men at the premises. He said the misunderstanding began when the complainant ignored remarks made by the group, which led one of the suspects to pull out a knife and swing it at the victim. Vijaya said acting on information, police arrested one of the suspects yesterday, and checks revealed that the individual had eight criminal records and three drug-related records.

items.title: Bank Negara: Malaysia's e-payment transactions jump 25pct to 18.4bil in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: E-payment transactions grew by 25 per cent to 18.4 billion, compared to 14.7 billion in 2024, with each Malaysian making 538 e-payments in 2025 on average, a 25 per cent increase from 432 in 2024. Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) said in its Annual Report 2025 that e-payment transactions rose by an annual average rate (CAGR) of 17 per cent between 2022 and 2025, making Malaysia consistently surpass the Financial Sector Blueprint 2022-2026 target of at least 15 per cent CAGR in average e-payment transactions per capita. It said card payments also recorded a healthy growth, with debit card transactions' growth almost doubling the pace of credit card transactions (20.5 per cent versus 10.9 per cent), while the average debit card ticket size was lower by 11.5 per cent to RM69 (2024: RM78).

items.title: Thailand denies cases of petrol theft involving Malaysian vehicles
items.summary: KOTA BHARU: The Thai-Asean Halal Tourism Industry Association (TAHTA) denies allegations that there have been cases of petrol being stolen from the tanks of Malaysian vehicles visiting southern Thailand. TAHTA president Aida Oujeh said there were indeed cases of petrol theft, but these involved Thai vehicles in Narathiwat during the recent Ramadan. Earlier, it went viral on social media that there were cases of petrol theft in southern Thailand involving Malaysian vehicles.

items.title: Penang DOSH clamps down on Bukit Minyak site after worker killed by falling plywood from crane
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 31 — The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) Penang has issued a Prohibition Notice and a stop-work order to the employer at a construction site in the Bukit Minyak Industrial Park following a fatal accident recently. He said the foreign worker later died at Hospital Seberang Jaya on March 24 due to severe injuries sustained in the fall. “The accident occurred at the construction site on March 19.

items.title: BNM says new Consumer Credit Commission will oversee BNPL sector amid rising debt and rapid expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The establishment of the Consumer Credit Commission is timely and important in safeguarding consumers through the regulation and supervision of previously unregulated nonbank credit and credit service providers, including buy now pay later (BNPL) providers. “The establishment of the commission will bring previously unregulated non-bank credit providers and credit service providers, including BNPL providers, under a formal regulatory and supervisory framework. “Correspondingly, total outstanding BNPL debt increased to RM4.9 billion as of end- December 2025 from RM3.8 billion in June 2025.

items.title: SPM 2025 levels up as results hit record high, more candidates earn certificates, grade average improves
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination results recorded the best achievement with a National Grade Average (GPK) index of 4.42 points compared to 4.49 points in 2024. Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said that a total of 366,435 candidates or 94.29 per cent were eligible to be awarded the SPM certificate in 2025 compared to 355,933 candidates in 2024. “Millions of congratulations to all teachers whose efforts have been expressed in the form of SPM results,” he said in a press conference announcing the 2025 SPM results here today.

items.title: Red Honda Civic chase at KLIA ends with arrest of local man carrying 31 criminal records
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — A police car chase in the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) area yesterday led to the arrest of a local man with 31 prior criminal records. KLIA police chief ACP M. Ravi said the 30-year-old suspect rammed several public vehicles in an attempt to escape while being pursued by a mobile patrol vehicle (MPV) in the incident at 4.30pm. He said MPV personnel conducting crime prevention patrols around the KLIA area attempted to stop a red Honda Civic, but the driver refused to pull over for inspection.

items.title: Johor KPDN intercepts suspicious lorry, seizes 800 litres of illicit diesel worth RM20,716
items.summary: Johor KPDN director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said in a statement that the Enforcement Patrol Unit (URP) detected a suspicious lorry and acted to detain and inspect the vehicle, at about 2pm. The IBC, she said, is believed to contain about 800 litres of diesel fuel. Lilis Saslinda said the lorry driver, in his 40s, failed to produce valid documents including a special permit for storing or purchasing diesel fuel and a statement was taken for investigation purposes.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh visits KLCC with mayor to check on unlicensed ‘photo touts’ preying on tourists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh last night visited the area around the Petronas Twin Towers and Suria KLCC with Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri TPr Fadlun Mak Ujud to check on the presence of unlicensed ‘photo touts’ operating at the landmark site. Yeoh said she spent more than an hour there and witnessed first-hand how the touts approached tourists to offer photography services. Authorities have pledged tighter enforcement, with Yeoh earlier announcing plans to install more closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras and increase patrols in the area.

items.title: Police confirm 10 arrests in Kulim factory trespass case, including journalist, amid tensions over migrant labour
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have confirmed the arrest of 10 men, including a portal journalist, to assist in investigations into alleged trespassing at a factory construction site in the Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP), Kulim last Wednesday. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said eight of them were detained last Saturday, while two others were arrested yesterday. He said the eight individuals detained earlier have been released on police bail, while the two arrested yesterday will be brought to court today for a remand order.

items.title: With crude prices soaring, Petronas steps up monitoring and calls for smarter energy use across the nation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) continues to closely monitor the nation’s fuel supply to help safeguard nationwide stability, amid the ongoing West Asia crisis. The national oil company also advises members of the public to avoid panic buying and hoarding of fuel as this would worsen the impact of this crisis. All these developments have impacted Malaysia’s fuel supply security,” it added.

items.title: Nurul Izzah shuts down viral chatter, insists she’s firmly holding on to PKR deputy president role
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Nurul Izzah Anwar has denied claims that she has stepped down as PKR deputy president. Speculation that Nurul Izzah had vacated the party’s deputy president’s post went viral on social media recently, sparking various reactions within the party. The daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was elected as the party’s deputy president for the 2025-2028 term in last year’s elections, defeating incumbent Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli in a straight fight.

items.title: Include us in RM1,000 tourism tax relief to boost domestic tourism, Malaysia’s budget hotels tell govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The Malaysian government should encourage more Malaysians to travel locally by giving them tax relief for hotel stays, an industry group has suggested. Malaysia Budget & Business Hotel Association (MyBHA) president Sri Ganesh Michiel said this proposed tax measure would help with the expected reduced number of overseas visitors due to the Middle East conflict. — File picture by Shafwan Zaidon Minimal impact on budget hotels now Sri Ganesh, whose association represents around 2,900 hotels rated three stars and below, said the Iran war’s impact on such budget hotels “remains minimal” for now.

items.title: Give us tax relief as a ‘shortcut’ targeted aid, Malaysia’s tourism industry urges govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The Malaysian government can provide tax relief and tax rebates to the local inbound tourism industry, which would be faster than going through the process of providing direct subsidies for fuel costs, the Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) said today. Under this proposed method, she proposed that the Inland Revenue Board provide tax relief or tax reductions during annual tax declarations, and said this should apply to legitimate or licensed tour agents and tourism transport operators. In the same press conference, Leong highlighted that tour buses and tour vans use diesel.

items.title: PM Anwar to chair economic council meeting on West Asia conflict tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to chair the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting tomorrow to discuss strategies to address the impact of the geopolitical conflict in West Asia on the country’s food security. Deputy Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Chan Foong Hin hopes the discussions will provide relief to agricultural industry players, particularly in the fisheries and crop cultivation sectors, which are currently facing major challenges due to current developments. Chan said the geopolitical conflict in West Asia is not merely a regional issue but has sparked a global chain reaction, including an energy crisis and a surge in oil prices, putting direct pressure on the economies of countries in Asia.

items.title: Transport Ministry moves to make intoxicated drivers pay compensation to victims’ families
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 30 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT) is drafting amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) to require those convicted of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs to pay compensation to victims’ families. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the compensation would be in addition to prison sentences under Act 333, in line with the ministry’s uncompromising stance against drink-driving, which endangers other road users. He said under Section 44 of the Act, any individual who drives under the influence of alcohol or drugs and causes death may, upon conviction, face imprisonment of not less than 10 years and not more than 15 years, as well as a fine of between RM50,000 and RM100,000.

items.title: Selangor reopens RM1,000 higher education study grant applications until April 14
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — The Selangor government has reopened applications for the Higher Education Institution (IPT) Study Grant until April 14 to help ease the financial burden of Selangor-born students pursuing higher education. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the initiative offers a RM1,000 aid to first-year students of IPTs recognised by the state government from the Level 4 Skills Certificate to Bachelor’s Degree levels. “This year, the state government is allocating RM5 million, up from the previous RM3 million, so that more students can enjoy this benefit.

items.title: Police: Investigation papers on duo accused of soliciting ‘protection money’ sent to prosecutors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The investigation paper on the case of two individuals from the Wangsa Maju Federal Territory Residents’ Representative Council (MPP), allegedly soliciting protection money from a business premises, has been sent to the deputy public prosecutor (DPP) today. Wangsa Maju Police chief SAC Mohamad Lazim Ismail said the police are now awaiting further instructions from the prosecution regarding the next course of action. “The investigation paper has been referred today.

items.title: ‘Prison is not enough’: Widow vows legal action after husband killed in Klang crash involving suspected drunk driver
items.summary: Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said she will take legal action to defend the rights of their three young children, aged between two and nine, who have lost both their father and the family’s main source of income. “I will take action. I am thinking about them,” she said when met at her family home in Kampung Repoh, Batu Kurau near here yesterday.

items.title: Government ready to roll out cloud seeding as prolonged heat grips several states nationwide
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) is prepared to implement intervention measures, including cloud seeding, to mitigate the impact of the prolonged hot weather currently affecting several states nationwide. Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the measures would be carried out based on prevailing needs, guided by data and technical reports from relevant agencies to ensure the impact of the weather phenomenon is effectively managed, particularly in sectors such as agriculture. Arthur said the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) would continue to play a key role in providing accurate weather information to support follow-up actions, while helping relevant agencies and the public make the necessary preparations.

items.title: Muar student awaiting SPM results dies in crash with bus at Parit Jawa traffic light junction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — An 18-year-old student awaiting his Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results died after colliding with a bus at the Parit Jawa traffic light junction in Muar, Johor, yesterday. Muar district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz, said preliminary investigations indicated the bus, coming from Batu Pahat towards Muar town, had stopped at the traffic lights. “As the lights turned green, the bus driver signalled a left turn when the student’s motorcycle collided with the left side of the bus.

items.title: Lorry driver in Batu Pahat pleads not guilty to slapping, punching and kicking wife for leaving home without permission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A 39-year-old lorry driver pleaded not guilty at the Magistrates’ Court in Batu Pahat, Johor, today to charges of assaulting his 40-year-old wife after she left their home without his knowledge. Mohamed Hamka Salleh, father of six, entered the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass, according to Harian Metro . Fearful of further harm, the victim lodged a police report at the Batu Pahat District Police Headquarters.

items.title: Give Malaysia’s tour buses 3,000 litres diesel subsidy, travel industry urges govt amid soaring prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The Malaysian government should offer diesel subsidies to tour buses and tour vans amid an 82 per cent jump in local retail diesel prices due to the Iran war, Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) urged today. MITA president Mint Leong said local tourism transport operators are struggling to cope with and absorb the increased costs from the diesel price hike, as most of their vehicles such as tour buses and tour vans use diesel. “MITA wishes to request to the government, if subsidies are given to the tourism industry, 3,000 litre per month for tour buses and 2,500 litre per month for tour vans,” she said in a press conference here, adding that this was a reasonable amount based on calculations from the local travel industry.

items.title: Over 50,000 devotees flock to Maran temple for Panguni Uthiram, police tighten safety measures
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 30 — Over 50,000 Hindu devotees, both local and international, are expected to visit the Sri Maranthandavar Temple in Sungai Jerik, Maran, for the Panguni Uthiram festival, which runs from today until Wednesday. Maran police chief Supt Wong Kim Wai said an estimated 10,000 vehicles are expected in the area during the period. The public is advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Simpang Bangas, continuing to Jalan Utama and Bandar Jengka.

items.title: Police confirm Victor Chin detained in Ops Viking, RM473m assets seized in money‑laundering crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Police have confirmed that businessman Victor Chin Boon Loong was among 10 individuals detained on suspicion of involvement in money laundering activities under Ops Viking, which was conducted between October 15 last year and January 13 this year. Bukit Aman AMLA (Anti-Money Laundering and Anti-Terrorism Financing) Investigation Unit chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abd Majid said Victor Chin was detained in December last year and has since been released on police bail, although he may be recalled if necessary. He said the operation was launched on October 14 last year based on intelligence on money laundering activities dating back to 2018, with a formal investigation opened on Oct 25 under Section 4(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613).

items.title: Malaysia’s tourism industry announces 70-80pc maximum price hike for tour buses, vans due to diesel pinch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Tour bus and tour van operators will be able to increase their prices by up to 80 per cent, the Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) announced today in a new price guideline effective from today. MITA president Mint Leong announced the new price guide, which the local tourism industry has stressed today is crucial for its survival as diesel prices continue to climb up in Malaysia. “Therefore with that, we announce today price increase for tour buses and tour vans.

items.title: Transport Ministry comes down hard: No compromise on drivers under influence after fatal Klang crash; prison, possible compensation for victims’ families
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 30 — The government will not compromise on any individual driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said, following a fatal crash in Klang involving a motorcyclist. Loke also noted that amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987, effective October 2020, provide for heavier penalties, including 10 to 15 years’ jail and fines of RM50,000 to RM100,000 for causing death while driving under the influence, with even stiffer sentences for repeat offenders. The ministry is also considering further amendments to require offenders to compensate victims’ families, in addition to serving prison sentences.

items.title: High‑stakes chase in Wang Kelian: Perlis police nab five, seize 510kg meth worth RM25.5m
items.summary: KANGAR, March 30 — Police arrested five individuals, including a married couple, and seized 510 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine worth an estimated RM25.5 million in three separate raids in Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit near Padang Besar last week. Hussein said police arrested the married couple and a male suspect from two cars they were travelling in along the main road in Wang Kelian during the first raid on March 24. Hussein said the arrest of the three individuals led to the capture of two more male suspects in two separate raids around Wang Kelian on March 27 and 28.

items.title: Court rules Papagomo guilty of sedition, RM4,000 fine imposed for post against King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, was fined RM4,000 in default four months’ jail by the Sessions Court here today after being found guilty of publishing seditious content deemed to incite hatred against the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, two years ago. On May 2, 2024, the former Umno Youth executive council member claimed trial for publishing seditious material on his Twitter (now X) account in relation to an issue involving the owner of KK Supermart & Superstore Sdn Bhd meeting Sultan Ibrahim. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Mohd Sabri Othman urged the court to impose a commensurate sentence, citing the accused’s background as a former police officer who should have had a better understanding of the law.
